behaviors.description: Migrations are applied in ascending numeric order determined by filename prefix.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (filesystem discovery -> filename parsing/sort -> executor), not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: no required real OS/network/subprocess beyond local filesystem and DB wiring. MOCK-HIDE: unit-only sort tests can pass while runner applies in wrong order due to discovery/execution integration bugs. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text only requires ordering by filename prefix, not subprocess/network/OS-event semantics; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI step exists.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp migrations directory with out-of-order files (e.g., 001_a.sql, 010_c.sql, 002_b.sql), run the runner against a local test Postgres, and assert execution order from side effects or _migrations rows is 001, 002, 010.

behaviors.description: A migration that has already been applied is not re-executed on a subsequent run.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when metadata persistence and migration execution interact across runs. REAL-INFRA: no mandatory subprocess/network behavior; local DB state is sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: mocking repository/executor can hide wrong SQL lookup or idempotency regression. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is about rerun idempotency via _migrations state, not OS/process/network effects; rejected Agentic/Workflow due no model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run migrations once on local test Postgres, record side effects, run again, and assert no migration SQL is executed again (no duplicate side effects and no duplicate _migrations entries).

behaviors.description: When a migration statement fails, its changes are rolled back automatically and no further migrations in the same run are attempted.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is transaction + control-flow interaction (executor, DB transaction, migration loop). REAL-INFRA: needs real DB transaction semantics, but not required subprocess/real external API. MOCK-HIDE: fake transaction layers often miss rollback semantics and partial-commit bugs. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text requires DB rollback/stop logic within one runner process, not subprocess/OS/network conditions; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI output involved.
behaviors.test_description: Use local Postgres with three migrations where second intentionally fails after making a write; run runner and assert: first migration committed, second fully rolled back, third not attempted, and metadata reflects only successful prior migrations.

behaviors.description: When two instances of the runner start at the same time against the same database, each migration is applied exactly once across both instances.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: race failures emerge only with concurrent instances interacting through real scheduling/locking timing. REAL-INFRA: explicitly requires separate processes starting simultaneously; subprocess/OS scheduling is part of failure boundary. MOCK-HIDE: in-process mocks/fakes usually serialize access and miss duplicate-apply races. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly says 'two instances ... start at the same time', which requires real process-level concurrency to reproduce reliably.
behaviors.test_description: Launch two runner processes concurrently against the same Postgres instance with identical migration set; wait for both to finish; assert every migration appears exactly once in _migrations and schema/data side effects exist exactly once.

behaviors.description: On the very first run against a fresh database, the _migrations table is created before any migration file is applied.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bug is in orchestration between bootstrap metadata creation and migration application. REAL-INFRA: local DB + filesystem wiring is enough; no mandatory subprocess/network behavior. MOCK-HIDE: unit tests of table-creation helper alone can miss runner calling order regressions. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text concerns first-run initialization order within runner, not OS/subprocess/network effects; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI stages are present.
behaviors.test_description: Against a brand-new local Postgres database and non-empty migrations directory, run runner once and assert _migrations exists before first migration bookkeeping/side effects (e.g., by verifying successful insert into _migrations tied to first migration and no pre-table failure).
